name: Magnesium (Citrate / Glycinate / Bisglycinate)
reason: Core sleep supplement (pre-bed 300–500mg) and daily hormonal support (split across day). Involved in 300+ enzyme reactions, cortisol regulation, and GLUT4 support. Author does 2–3g/day total split. Start with glycinate or bisglycinate (easiest on gut).

name: L-Theanine
reason: Core sleep supplement: 200–400mg 30 min before bed. Promotes relaxation without sedation. Part of the minimum sleep stack (Magnesium + L-Theanine + Apigenin).

name: Apigenin
reason: Core sleep supplement: 50mg 30 min before bed. Part of the minimum sleep stack. Sleep-regulating brain receptor modulator. Also used in DHT aromatase management protocols.

name: Vitamin D3 + K2 MK-7
reason: Core daily supplement: 5,000–10,000 IU D3 + 300–700mcg K2 MK-7. Fat-soluble — take with food. 86% of men with pattern hair loss are deficient. D3 supports Leydig STAR protein (testosterone synthesis) and VDR in hair follicle stem cells.

name: Zinc (Glycinate or Picolinate)
reason: Core daily mineral: 15–30mg with food. Required for steroidogenic enzymes and 5AR function. Classic human study: 20-week zinc restriction cut testosterone in young men. Monitor copper if supplementing long-term (add 1–2mg copper if zinc >25–30mg/day for weeks).

name: Selenium (Selenomethionine or Selenium Glycinate)
reason: Core daily mineral: 100–200mcg with food. Cofactor for all three deiodinases (T4→T3 conversion). Protects Leydig cells from oxidative damage during steroidogenesis. Do not exceed 140–200mcg/day (pro-oxidant above threshold).

name: B-Complex (with B6 caution)
reason: Core daily vitamin: supports methylation, energy, and steroidogenesis. Take with morning food. Caution: avoid daily high-dose isolated B6 (neuropathy risk at doses >50mg B6/day long-term).

name: Vitamin C (Ascorbic Acid)
reason: 3–5g/day baseline, split across meals. Leydig cell protection and cortisol reduction. Do NOT take at night — author experienced sleep disruption from evening Vitamin C.

name: Creatine Monohydrate (Creapure grade preferred)
reason: Core daily supplement: 10–15g/day split across meals. ATP regeneration, NADPH support, 5AR cofactor, potential DHT lever. Foundational for both performance and DHT optimization. Intra-workout glucose assists creatine uptake.

name: Boron (Glycinate, Citrate, or Calcium Fructoborate)
reason: 3–6mg each morning with food — MORNING ONLY. Lowers SHBG, increases free T and DHT, reduces hsCRP and TNF-α. Causes premature early waking if taken in the afternoon or evening.

name: Omega-3 (EPA + DHA fish oil)
reason: ~1g EPA + 1g DHA per day. Anti-inflammatory. Important note from KB: DHA inhibits 5-alpha-reductase — keep dose at recommended range; do not mega-dose if DHT optimization is a priority.

name: Valerian Root
reason: Optional sleep stack addition: 500mg 30 min before bed. Add after establishing baseline with Magnesium + L-Theanine + Apigenin. Sedative properties for relaxation and easier sleep onset.

name: Passion Flower
reason: Optional sleep stack addition: 200–400mg 30 min before bed. Add one supplement at a time after baseline stack. Calming nervous system effects.

name: Lemon Balm
reason: Optional sleep stack addition: 200–400mg 30 min before bed. Calming effect. Add one at a time to the sleep stack.

name: CBD Oil (2%)
reason: Optional sleep tool: 7 drops of 2% CBD taken 5 hours before bed (not at bedtime — has stimulating effect for 3–4h then calms). Author's personal use.

name: Dextrose or Cyclic Dextrin (intra-workout carbs)
reason: Intra-workout carb source: 30–50g in water + 2g sodium, started after first 1–2 heavy sets. Pure glucose/dextrose or cyclic dextrin only — not sucrose, not fructose, not maltodextrin with fructose.

name: R-ALA (R-Alpha Lipoic Acid — pure R-form)
reason: Post-workout supplement: 300mg with first post-workout meal. PDH activation (carb→mitochondria gate), extends GLUT4 window, AMPK activator, antioxidant recycler. R-form only (natural bioactive form); regular ALA is 50/50 R+S mix.

name: HMB (Ca-HMB or HMB-FA / BetaTOR)
reason: Post-workout: 1g with first post-workout meal. Activates mTOR (PI3K/Akt) and AMPK (LKB1) simultaneously — builds muscle and mitochondria without forcing a tradeoff. HMB-FA or BetaTOR form for faster absorption pre-training.

name: Taurine
reason: Post-workout: 2g with first post-workout meal. Cell volumization (independent anabolic signal), GABA-A (parasympathetic support), TGR5/bile acid signaling (T4→T3 local conversion). Also supports recovery and electrolyte balance.

name: Joint Stack: MSM + Glucosamine + Chondroitin
reason: Post-workout joint stack: MSM 2g + Glucosamine 2g + Chondroitin 1g with first post-workout meal. Connective tissue repair signaling peaks post-training. Take consistently, not just when joint pain is present.

name: Collagen Peptides
reason: 10–20g/day split AM + post-training. Supports hair follicle matrix, connective tissue, and gut lining integrity. Take with Vitamin C (1g) for enhanced collagen synthesis.

name: Glycine
reason: 3–5g pre-bed. Sleep quality support; also used in anti-anxiety and shift-worker DHT protocols. Can cause grogginess in sensitive individuals — lower dose if so.

name: LCLT (L-Carnitine L-Tartrate)
reason: 2–3g 30–60 min pre-training. Shuttles fat into mitochondria (CPT1), manages CoA availability, and upregulates androgen receptor density. Best taken with protein pre-workout.

name: Red Meat (Beef / Lamb / Bison — grass-fed preferred)
reason: Base of the androgen diet — consumed daily. Provides heme iron, zinc, B12, creatine, carnitine, saturated fat, and cholesterol for steroidogenesis. Grass-fed preferred for higher fat-soluble vitamin content. 85–90% of animal protein base.

name: Organ Meats / Beef Liver (grass-fed)
reason: 50–100g, 2–3 times per week. Most nutrient-dense food: vitamins A, B12, folate, copper, choline. Critical for methylation, steroidogenesis, and thyroid conversion cofactors.

name: Whole Eggs (pasture-raised)
reason: 3–10 eggs per day as tolerated. Provides cholesterol (steroid precursor), choline, fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, K, E), complete protein, selenium. Do not discard yolks. Also used for egg hair washing protocol (2–4 raw eggs).

name: Oysters / Shellfish (canned or fresh)
reason: Every other day: 1 can or 6 large oysters. Best natural food source of zinc, copper, selenium, and iodine — all critical for steroidogenesis, thyroid conversion, and 5AR function. Water-packed canned oysters are acceptable.

name: White Rice (jasmine or short-grain preferred)
reason: Primary post-workout starch: 100–200g hot freshly cooked. Must be eaten hot — do not cool or refrigerate before post-workout use (retrogradation creates resistant starch). Also used as general carb base throughout the week.

name: Potatoes (white or yellow)
reason: Post-workout starch: peel, boil, mash hot — fastest post-workout starch absorption option. Also general carb base. Always freshly cooked. Never cooled or reheated for post-workout window.

name: Oats (plain rolled — for fermentation protocol)
reason: PREP REQUIRED: soak, discard water, ferment 24h in raw A2 kefir/yogurt. Not a generic daily carb — use white rice and potatoes as primary starches. Oats only after proper preparation degrades phytic acid.

name: A2 Kefir / Goat Yogurt / Raw A2 Dairy
reason: Used as fermentation medium for oats (24h ferment). Also a general protein + probiotic source later in the day. Source matters: A2 milk, goat, sheep, or certified raw dairy. Avoid standard supermarket dairy from grain-fed A1 cows.

name: Bone Broth / Marrow Bones (grass-fed)
reason: 1–2 cups daily or several times/week. Provides collagen precursors (glycine, proline), gelatin, minerals, and gut lining support. Make from beef marrow bones/knuckles — simmer 12–24h in filtered water with a splash of apple cider vinegar.

name: Tallow / Ghee / Grass-fed Butter
reason: Exclusive cooking fats — used for all cooking and as fat source in meals. Zero seed/vegetable oils. Grass-fed butter/tallow provides vitamins A, D, E, K2, saturated fats for steroid hormone synthesis. Also: grass-fed beef tallow for scalp (hair protocol).

name: Celtic Sea Salt (unrefined)
reason: Sodium source for intra-workout drink: 2g per session in dextrose drink. Also for general cooking and meal prep. Celtic sea salt preferred (trace mineral content). Target ~2g sodium during training sessions.

name: Baking Soda (for meat prep)
reason: Used in meat prep protocol: soak meat in water + 5g baking soda per bowl for 1–3 hours before cooking. Raises pH, softens meat, pulls out oxidized fats. Also used to soak/wash produce.

name: Blue Light Blocking Glasses
reason: Wear 1–2 hours before bed every night. Blocks blue light wavelengths that suppress melatonin onset. Author uses them as part of the core sleep protocol. Also: lower all screen brightness simultaneously.

name: Earplugs (Loop Quiet or equivalent)
reason: Used nightly as part of sleep environment setup. Author uses Loop Quiet brand specifically. Any ambient noise disrupts sleep architecture and reduces deep sleep and REM time.

name: Blackout Curtains
reason: Complete light elimination during sleep. Any ambient light disrupts melatonin and sleep architecture. Author's preferred sleep environment tool. Alternative for travel: sleep mask.

name: Hygrometer / Room Thermometer
reason: Verifies bedroom temperature (target 15–20°C / 60–68°F) and humidity (target 40–60%). Core body temperature must drop to initiate deep sleep — higher temperatures prevent N3 entry. Author mentions as part of sleep environment optimization.

name: Cooling Mattress Pad / Orthopedic Mattress Topper
reason: Mentioned in the author's personal sleep tool stack. Helps maintain cooler sleep temperature. Secondary optimization after core sleep tools (blackout, earplugs, temperature) are in place.

name: Orthopedic Pillow
reason: Mentioned in the author's personal sleep tool stack for cervical support and sleep quality. Secondary optimization after core sleep tools are in place.

name: Derma Stamp or Derma Pen (0.5–1.0mm) — NOT roller
reason: Used for scalp microneedling every 7–14 days. MUST be stamp or pen — NOT a derma roller (rollers create micro-tears; stamps create clean punctures). 0.5–1.0mm needle depth for scalp. Apply treatment oils immediately after for 10–100× absorption.

name: Rosemary Essential Oil (100% pure)
reason: Used at 10–15% concentration in carrier oil blend for scalp. Applied 2–3×/week and post-microneedling. Ursolic acid + carnosic acid improve scalp circulation. Study: rosemary oil (1%) outperformed minoxidil (2%) for hair regrowth with zero side effects.

name: Castor Oil (cold-pressed, hexane-free)
reason: 40% base of carrier oil blend for scalp: thick base, growth stimulation via ricinoleic acid → PGE2 activation → anagen promotion. Applied 2–3×/week as part of hair oil blend protocol.

name: Carrier Oils for Hair Blend (coconut + olive/avocado + jojoba)
reason: Components of the hair carrier oil blend: coconut oil 30% (penetration, antimicrobial) + olive or avocado oil 20% (anti-inflammatory) + jojoba or grapeseed oil 10% (lighter texture). Mix with castor oil and rosemary EO. Store in dark glass bottle.

name: Grass-fed Beef Tallow (for scalp application)
reason: Applied to scalp 1–2×/week: melt slightly, massage into scalp, leave 2–6h or overnight, wash with egg or clay. Provides vitamins A, D, E, K2, stearic/palmitic acids, and cholesterol as local hormone synthesis precursor for the scalp.

name: Bentonite Clay (Aztec Secret / food grade)
reason: Used as scalp mask 1–2×/week (2–3 tbsp mixed with apple cider vinegar or water; 10–20 min contact; wash off with egg or water). ~50% silica — important for keratin synthesis. Also used internally in Hair Loss ebook (1 tsp in water daily, empty stomach, away from all supplements — binds nutrients).

name: Wooden Comb (bamboo / sandalwood / neem, wide-toothed)
reason: Use 2–3×/day, 50–100 strokes, gentle pressure. Eliminates static electricity (which causes breakage), distributes sebum from roots to ends, provides gentle scalp stimulation and circulation. Bamboo/sandalwood/neem wide-toothed preferred.

name: Silk or Satin Pillowcase
reason: Reduces friction on hair during sleep, reducing breakage and mechanical stress on follicles. Mentioned in the hair loss phased protocol (Phase 3 / Weeks 9–12+).

name: Shower Filter (multi-stage: KDF + activated carbon + vitamin C cartridge)
reason: Removes chlorine, chloramine, and heavy metals from shower water — all of which stress the scalp, alter local androgen sensitivity, and irritate hair follicles. Replace cartridge every 3–6 months. Mentioned in Hair Loss protocol Phase 1.

name: Red Light Therapy Device (660nm + 850nm panel)
reason: 3–4 sessions/week, 10–15 min per session at 6–12 inch distance. Requires minimum 30–50 mW/cm² at treatment distance. 660nm (red) + 850nm (near-infrared) combination. Mentioned in Hair Loss phased protocol Phase 2.

name: Dark Glass Bottle (for hair oil blend storage)
reason: Required for storing the rosemary/carrier oil blend. Light degrades essential oils rapidly — dark glass (amber or cobalt) is necessary to preserve potency. Mix and store the complete blend in this bottle.

name: Water Filter (activated carbon or reverse osmosis)
reason: Removes chlorine, chloramine, heavy metals, pesticides, and synthetic hormones from tap water. Minimum: activated carbon filter (Brita, under-sink carbon). Preferred: reverse osmosis or multi-stage system with glass storage.

name: Comprehensive Blood Work Panel (Phase 1 baseline)
reason: Phase 1 baseline panel: sex hormones, thyroid, metabolic, lipids, inflammation, micronutrients, liver, kidney, CBC with differential. Book through GP or private lab. Test fasting (10–12h). Do not test within 5–7 days of heavy lifting (ALT/AST falsely elevated).

name: Kestose (1-kestose ≥90% purity — NOT generic FOS)
reason: 5g twice daily with meals (after ramp). Prebiotic FOS that feeds Bacteroidota expressing 5AR genes → local T→DHT conversion in GI tract. Must be 1-kestose ≥90% purity — generic FOS is NOT a substitute. Ramp slowly: 1g → 2g → 3g → 5g per dose over 2+ weeks.

name: L. reuteri (BioGaia Gastrus or Osfortis — strains 6475 + 17938)
reason: 1–2 caps/day with food. Must include strain 6475 specifically for gut 5AR/testosterone benefits. BioGaia Gastrus or Osfortis confirmed to contain the right strains. Used in gut reset and DHT optimization protocols.
